page: Microsoft Azure NC24lds_xl_RTXPRO6000BSE_v6 is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine instance belonging to the NCdsRTXPRO6000BSEv6XL family. Powered by an x86_64 Intel processor, this server provides 24 dedicated vCPUs managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The system is configured with 72.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 3.0 GB of RAM per core, which positions it as a low-memory configuration relative to its compute capacity.

For acceleration, the instance features four integrated GPUs. Storage capabilities include 1980 GB of local disk space, and the virtual machine is premium storage capable, allowing for high-throughput disk configurations. The combination of dedicated Intel processing cores and multiple GPUs makes this instance suitable for parallelized computing tasks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the hardware profile represents a specialized balance of multi-GPU acceleration and local storage capacity, offset by a lower system memory allocation. This resource density makes it a cost-effective choice for workloads that require heavy GPU processing but do not demand massive system RAM. Appropriate workloads include GPU-accelerated machine learning inference, parallel data processing, rendering, and graphics-intensive applications.
description: Microsoft Azure NC24lds_xl_RTXPRO6000BSE_v6 is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine from the NCdsRTXPRO6000BSEv6XL family, designed for specialized parallel computing workloads. It features an x86_64 Intel processor with 24 dedicated vCPUs, 72.0 GB of system memory, and a resource density of 3.0 GB of RAM per core. The hardware configuration is equipped with four GPUs and 1980 GB of local storage, alongside premium storage capability. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance offers a cost-effective balance for resource-intensive tasks that prioritize GPU acceleration and local storage over high system memory. It is well-suited for workloads such as GPU-accelerated machine learning inference, rendering, and parallelized data processing.
